levelGuides.jacketFeature: extra warmth, easy pockets, and a forgiving relaxed fit
levelGuides.gearNote: Look for softer flex and stable control rather than aggressive speed.

levelGuides.jacketFeature: balanced insulation, stretch panels, and better ventilation
levelGuides.gearNote: Choose medium flex for more control while still staying comfortable.

levelGuides.jacketFeature: high waterproof rating, strong breathability, and technical layering
levelGuides.gearNote: Go for responsive gear that supports stronger carving and varied terrain.

skiTypes.name: Resort Groomer
skiTypes.recommendation: Choose narrower all-mountain skis that are stable and easy to turn on groomed runs.

skiTypes.name: Powder Day
skiTypes.recommendation: Choose wider skis with good float, plus a jacket with strong waterproof protection.

skiTypes.name: Freestyle Park
skiTypes.recommendation: Choose twin-tip skis or freestyle-friendly gear with flexible movement.

skiTypes.name: All-Mountain
skiTypes.recommendation: Choose versatile all-mountain skis that can handle mixed terrain and changing snow.

colorPalettes.name: Alpine Ivory
colorPalettes.styleTip: Keep accessories simple: silver goggles, white helmet, and one icy accent color.

colorPalettes.name: Black Diamond Minimal
colorPalettes.styleTip: Use texture instead of loud color: matte helmet, smoke lens, and structured outerwear.

colorPalettes.name: Icy Blue Performance
colorPalettes.styleTip: Blue looks sharp with chrome goggles and clean white base layers.

colorPalettes.name: Soft Rose Snow
colorPalettes.styleTip: Balance pink with neutral pants so the outfit feels polished, not childish.

colorPalettes.name: Nordic Earth Tone
colorPalettes.styleTip: Earth tones look expensive when paired with minimal accessories and warm lens goggles.
